transcript: All right. Hey everyone. My name is Mike and I'm the DevRel for Solana Mobile. Today I wanted to talk about our growing developer ecosystem and why the Solana dApp Store is the best place to launch an app. But first, for those who don't know, I'll explain what Solana Mobile is and what we're doing. Our mission is to bring crypto onto mobile and enable mass adoption. Mobile accounts for 63% of all web traffic and 73% of all e-commerce transactions. I think at this point most people understand that mobile is really important and it should be part of a product strategy. But most crypto experiences today are still only built for desktop. Why is that? Well, there are still major roadblocks in crypto's mobile landscape. Apple and Google are gatekeepers to distribution and they're hostile to crypto apps. This is a real problem for developers. I've talked to a lot of teams who have basically had to abandon their mobile apps because of this hostility. You can't justify time investing into a mobile app if you're not even sure it'll get approved, and you can't experiment with crypto app ideas and crypto features if it'll risk your app's approval. So innovation suffers and that's been holding back the whole ecosystem. So how are we solving this? In August, our team launched Seeker, a crypto-native mobile phone built to address this problem. We wanted to build an open mobile platform where crypto can thrive. So what makes Seeker crypto-native and how does it solve these problems? Couple of things. First, every Seeker has the Seed Vault, a built-in hardware wallet for secure storage and transaction signing. Any app on the device can connect to it and request signing with user approval. As a developer, this is huge. Your users can connect to the Seed Vault and receive a seamless signing experience all without ever leaving the app. It's like having an Apple Pay API available to you at all times, but for crypto transactions. And here's an experience—a demo of what that signing experience looks like on Jupiter. Here the user initiates a swap transaction. Seed Vault draws a bottom sheet. The user approves by double tapping and then confirming with a fingerprint scan. The bottom sheet is dismissed and the transaction is submitted. And we think this is one of the best signing experiences on mobile today. There's no jarring app switches and, um, no janky wallet browsers. It all happens within the app. And this is a big unlock for crypto apps. So that's the wallet experience. Now the second piece. Every Seeker ships with the Solana dApp Store, an alternative app store built to be a friendly home for crypto apps. Um, we have no hostile policies towards crypto and no 30% platform tax. As a developer, you can build a mobile app with crypto features and not have to worry about that being a risk to your app. And the dApp Store is where you'll find distribution. We have over 100,000 Seekers activated across multiple countries around the world. We have over 200 apps published in the dApp Store today. And these are crypto power users that are actively trying out downloading apps on the dApp Store. They're signing transactions, making purchases, playing games. To this date, we've had over a million app installs on the dApp Store and over $100 million in economic activity flowing through the Solana Mobile ecosystem. So for developers, the dApp Store should look like prime real estate. Unlike Apple and Google where your app is buried alongside millions of other apps, on the dApp Store you're competing against a much smaller pool of apps to a higher signal group of users. So if you're building an app, especially if it's in the early stages, this is one of the highest value user bases that you can tap into and find distribution. And we've heard a lot of success stories so far from teams launching on the dApp Store. Moonwalk Fitness is a gamified daily fitness, uh, app. And when they launched, they received 12,000 new user signups, um, just in their first week. And later they went on to achieve all-time highs for daily active users. We also have Perena, a DeFi app that provides yield on stablecoins. During their launch, they saw over 4,000 user deposits and over $13 million, uh, across all Seeker users. And these are just two of the many successful launches that we've seen so far. The feedback we've been getting from developers has been pretty clear. They see the dApp Store as a valuable platform to grow their app and find direct distribution to spendy crypto power users. So how do you actually publish onto the dApp Store? Let's talk about that process. First step, build an Android app. Seeker is an Android phone and so the dApp Store takes Android apps. There isn't any special framework or operating system you need to build for. It's just Android development. And what that means is you can use any standard Android framework like Kotlin, React Native, Flutter. Um, all that matters is that at the end of the day you produce an Android APK. And you don't actually even need a Seeker to develop for the dApp Store. You can test and build against any Android app or even an Android emulator. We provide development tools like the Mock Wallet so you can simulate what the bottom sheet signing experience looks like on the Seed Vault. And one of the most common questions we get is, can I publish a web app on the dApp Store? The answer is actually yes, yes you can. There's an easy process you can do to convert a PWA into an Android app. And to do that, you can use this open source tool called Bubblewrap developed by Google. Bubblewrap takes a PWA and then wraps that into an Android app that you can publish onto the dApp Store. It's just two CLI commands: init and then build, and then it'll generate you that Android APK. There's an app on the dApp Store called Crypto Fantasy League that actually uses Bubblewrap. And their PWA looks awesome because they optimized it for a mobile browsing experience. Using the app you can't really tell it's a PWA and it looks better than a lot of native apps. So the Bubblewrap route is definitely a viable strategy to get on the dApp Store quickly without having to build a mobile app from the ground up. And after you finish building your app, the last step is to submit it to the dApp Store. To do this, we recently launched the Publishing Portal, a website where you can sign up for a developer account and handle the entire app submission flow through a nice web UI. On the portal, you'll find a form where you can enter your app details, add any screenshots or previews, and then upload your APK and submit your app. And finally, after you've submitted your app, all that's left is just app review. How app review works is our team will take a look at the submission, make sure the metadata and the details are correct. We'll do a light QA check. And if all is good, we'll approve the submission and your app will be submitted to the App Store. We want this process to be as frictionless and simple as possible while also maintaining a bar for quality. So this is a pretty good idea, um, or now you have a pretty good idea of how to build for the dApp Store. For this last section, I wanted to talk about some upcoming opportunities to jump into the Solana Mobile ecosystem. First announcement, we're launching a grants program. These grants will cover a variety of things like developer tooling, SDKs, public goods, and also app ideas. You can scan the QR code or go to solanamobile.com/grants. And on there you'll find all the information about this program. Um, you'll find an application form to apply for a grant and also a list of existing RFPs. Definitely check this out. And the last announcement, we're going to be launching our second mobile hackathon in Q1 of 2026. Just like last time, this is going to be an opportunity for developers to build an app and compete, uh, and win prizes like cash and also featured placements on our dApp Store. So if you win, your app can get higher visibility. Stay tuned for more information here. And here are just some resources. So everything I talked about on in this presentation, everything about, um, our wallet SDKs, how to publish on the dApp Store, documentation, that can all be found on docs.solanamobile.com. And if you're in the audience and you're thinking about building an app or the dApp Store sounds interesting to you, maybe you don't even have an app yet, maybe you just have a web app and you're interested in Bubblewrap, um, or maybe you already have an existing app that's on the Play Store. Uh, please come find me or any of our team members. Uh, we have a booth downstairs where we are giving away free Seekers. Um, so come by, find us, talk to us. Um, we're happy to answer your questions and help you guys get onto the dApp Store. Thank you so much.

enrichment.generated_query: Solana Mobile Seeker official announcement blog documentation Solana Breakpoint 2025

sources.title: Solana Mobile Blog
sources.snippet:
Solana Mobile Blog
[![Solana Mobile Logo](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e435501/68fab532460012b81e435533_Solana%20Mobile.svg)](https://solanamobile.com)
[Home](https://solanamobile.com)[Seeker](https://solanamobile.com/seeker)[SKR](https://solanamobile.com/skr)[Developers](https://docs.solanamobile.com/)[Grants](https://solanamobile.com/grants)[Blog](https://www.solanalabs.com/)[Order Now](https://store.solanamobile.com/)
From Saga to Seeker: Solana's Mobile's Smartphone Revolution
After the breakout success of Solana Saga, here's how Solana Mobile's next device —Seeker —is taking web3 mobile global.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e43556b_cropped-Screenshot%202025-01-15%20at%2010.44.45%E2%80%AFAM.png)
*By*[*Emmett Hollyer*](https://x.com/m_it)*
‍*
When[Solana Mobile](https://solanamobile.com/)launched its first device in 2023 —Solana Saga —web3 mobile was a huge unknown. Skeptics questioned whether the world needed another smartphone manufacturer. Traditional tech reviewers dismissed it, with MKBHD famously labeling Saga the "biggest bust of 2023."
‍But then, things changed. BONK happened. Rewards happened. It was like the entire Solana community came together to get behind the mission of web3 mobile. We sold over 15,000 devices in a single day. The Solana Saga*sold out.*
‍As we prepare to launch our second device —[Solana Seeker](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MjQrbh4gaQM)— in mid-2025, it's become clear that we aren't just building another phone. We're building the infrastructure that will make cryptocurrency accessible and practical for everyday mobile users. And with over Seeker 150,000 preorders waiting to get in the hands of the Solana community already, that new paradigm is getting closer to reality every day.
‍The crypto landscape has long been defined by compromise when it comes to UI. Users toggle between multiple apps, navigate clunky interfaces, and accept subpar mobile experiences as the cost of participating in Web3.
‍Meanwhile, the existing duopoly of Apple and Google's app stores has created an environment where crypto applications struggle to reach users, hampered by restrictive policies and prohibitive revenue shares that scrape 30% off the top of all app revenues.
‍Our journey with Saga proved there was a better way. Even when initial sales were modest during the bear market of 2023, something remarkable happened: developers kept building. Teams who saw the potential of mobile-first crypto experiences created applications specifically for the Solana Mobile dApp store. When the market turned, Saga didn't just sell out, it validated our thesis that users were hungry for a mobile device that put crypto first.
‍Now, with Seeker, we're taking that vision global. Available worldwide at a more accessible $500 price point, Seeker represents the evolution of our initial concept. It's lighter, brighter, packed with new features like the Seed Vault Wallet and an updated dApp Store. But perhaps more importantly, it's a gateway to an ecosystem that's maturing at a phenomenal pace across digital assets, payments, DeFi, DePIN, AI, and gaming —and whatever the Solana ecosystem dreams up next.
‍[![Embedded YouTube video](https://img.youtube.com/vi/UHzdMjSwVvo/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UHzdMjSwVvo)
‍The heart of this ecosystem is our dApp store, where developers keep 100% of their revenue –a stark contrast to the 30% taken by Apple and Google. This isn't just about developer economics; it's about new business models that can reward users directly. When developers don't have to give away a third of their revenue to platform gatekeepers, they can create innovative reward systems and incentive structures that benefit their communities.
‍We're also addressing one of the most persistent challenges for web3 mobile: wallet integration. Our new Seed Vault

sources.snippet:
Solana Mobile Blog
[![Solana Mobile Logo](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e435501/68fab532460012b81e435533_Solana%20Mobile.svg)](https://solanamobile.com)
[Home](https://solanamobile.com)[Seeker](https://solanamobile.com/seeker)[SKR](https://solanamobile.com/skr)[Developers](https://docs.solanamobile.com/)[Grants](https://solanamobile.com/grants)[Blog](https://blog.solanamobile.com/)[Order Now](https://store.solanamobile.com/)
Introducing Solana Seeker, the Definitive Web3 Mobile Device
Here's everything you need to know about the latest device from Solana Mobile.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e435566_cropped-Screenshot%202025-01-14%20at%203.32.28%E2%80%AFPM.png)
The wait is over. Chapter Two has been revealed! Introducing…[**Solana Seeker**](http://solanamobile.com).
‍At the Token2049 conference in Singapore, Solana Labs co-founder Anatoly Yakovenko and Solana Mobile GM Emmett Hollyer unveiled the Solana Seeker device, offered a tour of its features, and laid out a roadmap for the future for web3 mobile.
‍“We founded Solana Mobile with the mission of taking crypto mobile,” explained Yakovenko. “To achieve that, we needed to make Seeker more accessible, more affordable, and built from the ground up to integrate its hardware and software for web3. With features like the new Seed Vault Wallet and the updated Solana dApp Store, we think Seeker will be the definitive web3 mobile device.”
‍[![Embedded YouTube video](https://img.youtube.com/vi/MjQrbh4gaQM/0.jpg)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=MjQrbh4gaQM)
‍With over 150,000 preorders for Solana Seeker from all over the world awaiting the device —which is set to ship in mid-2025 —let’s take a first look at some of Seeker’s hardware, new features and updates.
‍## **Seed Vault Wallet**
‍![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e435565_6786ae77909855c48378479c_AD_4nXcDRFGuHegFDTT-5ZUW105ZdSmK3saphTyp1aUODks_Gv6moVQ-Y89If4GHggLXJ6orCF0de9QxxtScsgbBxhb2We1tWbeP4mXpMPKIcbM1vCVpz-BxLi5hMOpELASNnkwnw6F-RQ.png)
‍Created exclusively for the Seeker in partnership with Solflare, Seed Vault Wallet is a mobile-first crypto wallet that’s natively integrated with the device’s built in hardware Seed Vault to ensure a seamless Web3 experience —featuring doubletap transactions and streamlined account management.
‍## **dApp Store 2.0**
‍![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e435563_6786ae77a38c636c14b41edf_AD_4nXfywIzoRvP56OEqXVRJQC6NN0TCcuw_VPiad0vAyXUgZGMiW-UQu7sXA67uZLOFFfX6gy0pkELcIWMcwQHISJ8fRgjIok4-5fC7-cukdYL9Z6UCifpz225safD1CdzYT7dXyvu6ow.png)
‍The updated Solana dApp Store will feature the latest applications across payments, DeFi, DePIN, NFTs, and gaming —plus exclusive dApps only available on Solana Mobile! Plus an all-new rewards tracker widget and improved discoverability for 100+ decentralized apps.
‍## **Seeker Genesis Token**
‍![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e435564_6786ae77bf23f1106d0032c2_AD_4nXe6w1x_tzZeWgWtePQS_mUdXHFYwXolvyLSbCTbYKCtPmlq5HTDsYYSW7ABJcObpbglnWyAUu_oFiznZ9saLkue5vvpF6zLmfn8r06hUKWDrA5Gu2Yl56_8VcA_3pZIsdBFlig0rg.png)
‍A unique, non-transferable NFT that provides the key to unlocking rewards, exclusive access, offers, and content from leading projects throughout the Solana ecosystem
‍## **Enhanced Hardware**
‍![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/68fab532460012b81e43552a/68fab532460012b81e435578_AD_4nXe6c0d40UqM19CDpQjU2h-YcC2V2y2USrD5SeXIJjnbYdSw-Z4AU3t7OGLJFB9VAvswm7R_6vEdIK5f6ky5HMP93Xsm1x5Vzc5jL6bf2ssqR6LMxa4oISPf4gsMKtMTNfi3-lCNfw.png)
‍The Seeker will feature lighter weight, brighter display, improved camera, and extended battery life as compared to its predecessor, and all at a much more affordable price point.
‍### Solana Seeker is scheduled to ship in mid-2025, and preorders are[available now.](http://solanamobile.com)
‍[![Solana Mobile Logo](https://cdn

tags.impact_reason: The Solana Mobile update provides a developer-friendly environment for launching crypto apps, which has the potential to drive further development of the ecosystem.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose the 'Seed Vault' hardware chip as the Semantic Anchor because it's the physical heart of the Solana Mobile story, representing a secure, crypto-native foundation that breaks free from the hostile Apple/Google duopoly. It is both the key and the vault. I included the diegetic text '100%' as a holographic projection to visually represent the core developer benefit mentioned in the standfirst: reclaiming full revenue from the old gatekeeper system.
visual_idea.description: A sleek, monolithic smartphone made of polished obsidian floats heroically in a dark, cavernous space. The phone is partially transparent, revealing its glowing inner-workings. At its core, the 'Seed Vault' chip pulses with a brilliant, warm light, like a protected crystal. This light pierces the gloom, projecting a holographic '100%' onto the surface of a massive, crumbling concrete wall in the deep background, representing the broken duopoly gatekeepers.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A sleek, monolithic smartphone made of highly polished obsidian, reflecting subtle light glints, floats heroically in a vast, dark, cavernous space. The phone's surface is partially transparent, revealing its intricate, glowing inner-workings, rendered with crystalline silicon aesthetics. At its core, the 'Seed Vault' chip pulses with an intense, brilliant, warm light, resembling a protected, radiant crystal. This powerful light dramatically pierces the surrounding gloom with volumetric light shafts and god rays, projecting a crisp, holographic '100%' onto the rough, textured surface of a massive, crumbling concrete wall in the deep background, visually representing the broken duopoly gatekeepers. The scene is composed for a vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, emphasizing height, epic scale, and significant negative space at the top, making the smartphone feel monumental and imposing. Cinematic volumetric lighting casts dramatic shadows and highlights, creating a moody, high-contrast atmosphere. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic volumetric lighting, subtle film grain, deep depth of field, high contrast, studio lighting.

The surrounding dark, out-of-focus phone components frame the shot, emphasizing the chip as a precious and powerful technological jewel.

The letters 'SEED VAULT' are intricately etched and subtly glowing with an internal light on the chip's crystalline silicon surface. We see highly detailed, intricate, crystalline silicon pathways and glowing micro-circuitry deeply etched into its surface. The chip itself is the primary light source, emitting powerful, cinematic volumetric lighting that creates beautiful caustics and refractions within its multifaceted structure. The surrounding dark, out-of-focus phone components, featuring a polished obsidian texture, elegantly frame the shot, emphasizing the chip as a precious, powerful, and monolithic technological jewel. The composition is perfectly square (1:1), with strong symmetry and a central, tight macro focus, filling the entire frame with rich texture and detail, bathed in dramatic contrast and deep, enveloping shadows. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, ultra-detailed, cinematic contrast.

editorial.headline: Solana Seeker Breaks Mobile Duopoly: 100k Active Units, $100M Volume
editorial.standfirst: Solana Mobile’s 'Chapter 2' device has shipped, delivering a gatekeeper-free ecosystem where developers keep 100% of revenue and access high-value institutional users.
key_takeaways: 100,000 Active Devices: Seeker units are live and transacting, with 1 million+ dApp installs since the August 2025 launch.
$100M Economic Activity: The ecosystem has generated nine-figure volume, validating the thesis of a 'spendy' crypto-native mobile user base.
Zero-Fee Distribution: The Solana dApp Store offers a tax-free alternative to Apple and Google’s 30% cut, plus a new Q1 2026 Hackathon incentive.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The mobile crypto thesis has transitioned from pre-order speculation to hard metrics. With the **Solana Seeker** officially shipping in August 2025, the network has successfully deployed over 100,000 active devices, creating a tangible wedge in the Apple-Google duopoly. For years, the industry has suffered from a distribution bottleneck: while mobile accounts for 63% of web traffic, crypto apps have been stifled by hostile app store policies and a prohibitive 30% revenue tax. Solana Mobile’s strategy to bypass these gatekeepers is no longer theoretical—it is generating $100 million in economic activity across a fleet of crypto-native devices.

For developers and investors, the Seeker represents a shift from low-signal mass markets to high-signal power users. Unlike the saturated iOS and Play Stores, the **Solana dApp Store** offers prime real estate to a concentrated audience of 100,000 users who are actively looking to transact. Early movers are already capitalizing on this density. **Moonwalk Fitness**, a gamified health app, secured 12,000 new user signups in its first week on the store. Similarly, **Perena**, a stablecoin infrastructure protocol, attracted over $13 million in deposits directly from Seeker users. These figures confirm that while the user base is smaller than Big Tech's, the conversion rate and economic value per user are significantly higher.

The hardware itself resolves the critical UX friction of mobile crypto: signing transactions. The Seeker’s **Seed Vault** integrates a hardware wallet directly into the device, allowing users to approve swaps and transfers with a double-tap and fingerprint scan. This 'Apple Pay for Crypto' experience removes the need for jarring app switches or clunky wallet browsers, enabling seamless interaction within dApps. Furthermore, the barrier to entry for deployment has been lowered; developers can port existing Progressive Web Apps (PWAs) to the dApp Store using **Bubblewrap**, bypassing the need to build native Android apps from scratch while still accessing system-level features.

Looking ahead, Solana Mobile is doubling down on ecosystem growth with a new grants program and a confirmed hackathon for Q1 2026. With the hardware in the wild and the **dApp Store** operating with a 0% platform fee, the infrastructure is now in place for a new class of mobile-first crypto applications that are economically unfeasible on traditional platforms.
